daily_brief: Russia launched one of its largest aerial assaults on Ukraine in months overnight, killing at least 16 civilians across multiple cities including Kyiv and Dnipro with 656 drones and 73 missiles targeting civilian and energy infrastructure [BBC, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. President Zelensky urgently appealed for additional US Patriot missile supplies. Meanwhile, fragile diplomatic efforts continue in the Middle East: despite President Trump announcing that Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to cease hostilities, clashes persisted in southern Lebanon overnight, with both sides exchanging fire [BBC, France 24, The Hindu]. Reports emerged of Trump-Netanyahu tensions over the arrangement's implementation. In technology, AI firm Anthropic filed for an IPO at a valuation approaching $1 trillion [France 24], while SoftBank announced €75 billion in AI infrastructure investment in France at the Choose France summit [RFI]. Denmark's Mette Frederiksen secured a third term as prime minister after months of coalition negotiations, forming a centre-left minority government amid ongoing US pressure over Greenland [BBC]. In East Africa, Kenya's proposed US Ebola quarantine facility sparked deadly protests in Nanyuki, with two people killed and courts ordering disclosure of facility details [BBC, Al Jazeera].

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Massive Overnight Strike on Ukrainian Cities, Killing at Least 16
bullets: Russia fired 656 drones and 73 missiles (ballistic, cruise, and anti-ship) at cities across Ukraine, killing at least 16 people including two children
Dnipro suffered 11 deaths including an 8-year-old boy; Kyiv reported 5 deaths with a 24-storey apartment building collapse trapping residents
Russia's defence ministry said strikes targeted civilian infrastructure and energy facilities as retaliation for Ukrainian attacks on occupied territory
President Zelensky urgently called for US Patriot missile supplies, noting shortages exacerbated by the US-Israeli war against Iran

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed casualty reporting with Zelensky quotes on need for Western support

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized days of Russian warnings and foreign diplomats ignoring evacuation calls

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Ukrainian Foreign Minister calling Putin a 'war criminal and loser'

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on Putin facing setbacks and discontent in Russia

top_stories.headline: Israel-Hezbollah Clashes Continue Despite Trump-Brokered Partial Ceasefire Agreement
bullets: President Trump announced Israel and Hezbollah agreed to stop fighting after he spoke with both sides; Lebanon confirmed Hezbollah accepted US proposal for mutual cessation of attacks
Israeli PM Netanyahu confirmed agreement but warned strikes on Beirut would continue if Hezbollah attacks persist; Israeli forces would still operate in south Lebanon
Overnight clashes continued with Hezbollah targeting Israeli tanks and Israel intercepting two projectiles from Lebanon
Axios reported Trump called Netanyahu 'crazy' and accused him of putting Iran peace talks at risk

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Factual reporting on ceasefire terms and ongoing violations

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump-Netanyahu tensions and 'crazy' comment from Axios report

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed diplomatic process through Lebanese president's office

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Framed as Trump weakening Netanyahu by forcing delay of Beirut attack

top_stories.headline: Two Killed in Kenya Protests Against US Ebola Quarantine Facility
bullets: Two people died from gunshot wounds during protests in Nanyuki against a planned 50-bed US Ebola isolation centre at nearby Laikipia Airbase
The facility would treat US citizens affected by the DRC Ebola outbreak; Kenya has recorded no cases
High Court extended orders halting the facility and ordered the government to disclose agreement details, health assessments, and protocols
President Ruto defended the plan, saying Kenya had 'deployed every arsenal' to protect the country

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed on-ground reporting of deaths and protest circumstances

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized court orders requiring government transparency on facility details

top_stories.headline: Anthropic Files for IPO at Nearly $1 Trillion Valuation Amid AI Investment Surge
bullets: Anthropic, maker of AI assistant Claude, submitted confidential IPO filing to the SEC and could debut on markets by year-end
The company is valued at approximately $965 billion, making it one of the largest tech IPOs in history
Filing comes as SoftBank announced €75 billion AI infrastructure investment in France at Choose France summit
France secured total of €93 billion in foreign investment pledges with AI and data centres accounting for bulk of commitments

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Straightforward business reporting on valuation and timeline

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Contextualized within broader Choose France summit and Macron's economic strategy

top_stories.headline: Denmark's Frederiksen Forms Coalition Government After Months of Negotiations
bullets: Social Democrat leader Mette Frederiksen will form centre-left minority coalition with Socialist People's Party, Radikale Venstre, and Moderates
Coalition follows inconclusive March election where Social Democrats won most votes but their weakest result since 1903 (21.9%, 38 seats)
Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister amid ongoing US pressure over Greenland
New cabinet to be presented Wednesday after meeting with King Frederik X

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's Greenland ambitions as context for coalition timing

additional_stories.headline: Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity with 3-10 year prison sentences, prompting community fears
Five Mozambicans killed in xenophobic attacks in South Africa's Mossel Bay, prompting repatriation of hundreds
Hungary's new PM Peter Magyar moves to oust Orbán-era president Tamás Sulyok through constitutional amendment
UK wins international arbitration case, will not pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum deportation scheme
Myanmar president Min Aung Hlaing makes first foreign visit to India, meeting PM Modi on trade and border security
WHO Director-General visits DRC as Ebola outbreak reaches 321 confirmed cases, calls for community cooperation
Gulf states arrested over 1,000 people for Iran war-related social media posts, Amnesty International reports
French astronauts Thomas Pesquet and Arnaud Prost set for 2027 space missions including first crewed visit to commercial station
SK Hynix announces plans to double wafer capacity within five years to meet surging AI chip demand
Trump signs proclamation adjusting Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum and copper derivative products
